classification.domain: LLM security for academic review systems

topic.what: Co-evolutionary adversarial training framework defending LLM-based peer review systems against adversarial hidden prompts
topic.how: Jointly optimizes Generator (GRPO-trained attacker) and Defender (DPO-trained reviewer) through iterative minimax game where attacker learns to craft injection prompts and defender learns to resist manipulation
topic.why_matters: Adversarial hidden prompts embedded in paper submissions can inflate review scores and accept flawed papers, undermining scholarly integrity and peer review fairness

characteristics.context: Most critical metric for peer review systems measuring correct paper ordering by quality under adversarial manipulation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of rejected papers wrongly accepted after manipulation
characteristics.context: Lower FPR indicates stronger defense against adaptive attacks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of accepted papers wrongly rejected
characteristics.context: Lower FNR ensures legitimate submissions not unfairly penalized

characteristics.what_itasures: Baseline robustness of DeepReviewer-14B against zero-shot attacks
characteristics.context: DeepReviewer-14B shows strongest baseline robustness among tested models

characteristics.what_it_measures: FPR reduction from baseline to SafeReview
characteristics.context: Measures overall defense improvement against GRPO attacks

not_for: proprietary API-based reviewers without fine-tuning capability
requires: Training infrastructure for GRPO and DPO

novelty: First co-evolutionary framework for defending peer review against adversarial hidden prompts
GRPO+DPO hybrid training combining attack evolution and defense strengthening
Cross-architecture generalization demonstrating robustness across different model families
Benign performance preservation while achieving robustness
open_problems: Investigating attack transferability across different reviewer models
Adapting defense for proprietary API-based reviewers (GPT-4, Claude)
Generalizing to diverse academic domains beyond Computer Science
Robustness against subtle non-instruction-based semantic perturbations
